channel.display-name: Weird or What? With William Shatner
The Yellow Couch with Jeremy Lynch
A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: Labyrinth
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Sarah must solve a labyrinth to rescue her baby brother when he is taken by the Goblin King.

programme.title: The Sentinel
programme.desc: Compromised by his affair with the first lady, a veteran secret service agent is framed as a potential presidential assassin and must rely on a hostile former prot‚g‚ to flush out the real culprit.

programme.title: Lars and the Real Girl
programme.desc: Lars Lindstrom is an akwardly shy young man in a small town who finally brings home the girl of his dreams to his brother and sister-in-law's home. The only problem is that she's not real - she's a sex doll.

programme.title: No Way Out
programme.desc: A morally upright naval hero accepts a position with the Secretary of Defense and his somewhat overzealous assistant. Things become a bit sticky when the Secretary of Defense becomes involved in the murder of a beautiful Washington, D.C. party girl.

programme.title: Push
programme.desc: Superhumans defy a government plot to harness and abuse their powers. Chris Evans and Dakota Fanning (the TWILIGHT films) team in a pulse-pounding paranormal thriller.

programme.title: A Guide to Recognizing Your Saints
programme.desc: A coming-of-age drama about a young man (Shia LaBeouf) growing up in 1980s New York who believes he has been saved from the culture of violence and drugs by various so-called saints.

programme.title: Sky Kids
programme.desc: As a pair of schoolboys sneak a look around an empty private aircraft, two criminals take off with the kids still inside. Upon discovering the men are dangerous gangsters, the horrified lads inadvertently become embroiled in the mobsters's scheme.

programme.title: Bottle Shock
programme.desc: The story of the early days of California wine making featuring the now infamous, blind Paris wine tasting of 1976 that has come to be known as "Judgment of Paris". Feat: Chris Pine, Alan Rickman & Bill Pullman.

programme.title: Noah
programme.desc: Russell Crowe stars as Noah in the timeless story of courage, sacrifice, and hope. This visually stunning, action-adventure is hailed as "one of the most unforgettable Biblical epics ever put on film." *Richard Roeper, Chicago-Sun-Times

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Slugfight / Izomborg
programme.desc: The campers must travel back in time to prevent Grandpappy Black Butt from stealing the deed.

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: The Storm Hawks are stranded in the dreaded Black Gorge, from which no airship has ever returned. Now the only one who can lead them through the unspeakable dangers to recharge their power crystal is Stork!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Depart
programme.desc: With the Autobot ship ready to leave, Hot Shot, supported by the rest of the team, refuse to let the children accompany them. But then the minicons refuse to allow the ship to depart unless the kids are on board.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Returning to the Deeps of Orothe to find its Dreamstone, the kids are horrified to see that the ocean realm has been attacked. A Giant Squid - The Guardian Hyren of Orothe – is busily sealing the city's leaking domes.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Eclipsalypso / Apocalypse Squirt
programme.desc: McGee summons the Moon Dervish who will bring the Apocalypse unless a sasquatch dances for him.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The Metajets are assigned a cushy mission: to protect the Royal family of a small country that has recently joined forces with the World Council in an effort to rid themselves of unwanted rebels

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: Competing against the strict and proper Rex Guardians for a powerful Phoenix Stone, the Storm Hawks prove that old-fashioned rules belong in the history books - not in the skies of battle.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Gore at War
programme.desc: Making a comeback is what Gore does best, but will his finisher be enough to vanquish Varis?

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: The Street Sharks attempt to foil a plot to genetically alter Fission City's citizens by means of a chemically altered water supply.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Bugging Out
programme.desc: The fate of LINK VRAINS hangs in the balance as Playmaker faces off against one of Hanoi’s top lieutenants!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Slaybells / Smells Like Holidays
programme.desc: The kids almost ruin the biggest monster holiday of the year!

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Johnny is so focused on the upcoming championship race, that he allows it to interfere with his concentration during a Metajets mission, resulting in the team endangering a day-care filled with small children.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Roots of Evil
programme.desc: Spectre knows Blue Angel's entire life story, and he’s going to give it a sad ending!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bite of the Buttsquat / Sword of Ittibiticus
programme.desc: No one believes McGee's suspicion that the suddenly ultra-cool Buttsquat is a campyre.
programme.sub-title: Bite of the Buttsquat / Sword of Ittibiticus

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The team is in London to see their friend Razor in concert. During the show, the Anarchitechs shut down power to the entire City, signaling their grand return.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: The Monsters have taken over the show and are throwing an epic slumber party to watch the best brawls and #unforgettablefails… but when RoBro challenges Macho to a Mashdown, they’re all in for a rude awakening!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: The Posse is battling with Skor over a crystal near the local airport, when the crystal slips out of both their hands and flies into a suitcase being loaded onto a passenger jet. The Posse springs into action to recover the crystal.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: When Johnny saves a fellow pilot from a fiery crash during a practise run, it leads to a reluctant friendship with the son of Carmone Fontaine, head of the Soaring Heights organized crime syndicate.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Blue’s Back!
programme.desc: Blue Angel continues her duel against an AI whose prime directives are: duel, decimate, delete!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Base
programme.desc: Alexis, Rad and Carlos introduce themselves to Optimus Prime, Hot Rod and First Aid, and get a condensed history of the conflict. Meanwhile, on the moon, Megatron plots to capture the minicons that are now appearing all over earth.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Runaway
programme.desc: Understanding that the minicons want to be in charge of their own destinies, the kids sneak the new team out of the base. Thrust finds them, but so do the Autobots who save the day. It's agreed the minicons should not be forced to fight.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Meet The Gretch's Parents / Now with 100% More Portal
programme.desc: McGee and Squirt are flabbergasted when Gretchen's parents turn out to be the famous action-star couple, the Gritchersons! The kids discover an interdimensional portal to travel back and forth between parallel Lakebottom dimensions.
programme.sub-title: Meet The Gretch's Parents / Now with 100% More Portal

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Messy Bessie / Nanny Num Nums
programme.desc: The campers build a race car to help Sawyer relive his pro-racing days.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Past, Part 1
programme.desc: Enthusiastic young Sideswipe, Blurr's hero-worshipper, joins the team and Optimus tasks Hot Shot with training him. Wheeljack also arrives on earth, bent on revenge for Hot Shot seemingly abandoning him during a battle on Cybertron.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Big Top Terror / Slimal Fear
programme.desc: The campers summon Armand's old rival, Doofus the clown who vows to destroy the campers.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: The KFDP learns of a MASSIVE CRYSTAL located somewhere in the middle of the desert. Edgar realizes that it’s actually quite a far distance outside of town. The dinos load up their DUV’s and head for an impromptu road trip.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Stage Fright / Frankenfixer
programme.desc: The Bottom Dwellers stage Armand's awful play but a jealous Suzi unleashes a curse on the production.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Meta-Squadron has managed to capture Fly-Girl and Crusher, forcing Raven to offer a trade - the release of his agents for the return of a missing Metajets officer

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: When Polly peers into an ancient chest in the museum’s storage room, an other-worldy glow envelopes her, and Polly finds herself with a strange and horrible affliction… she becomes a WERE-DINO! A hideous half-human, half-dino creature!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: In the transitional zone between realms, Tony, Edyn and Strag are on route to Paradwyn to get its Dreamstone when a panicked Drowl comes bounding through the realm's tall, waving grasses – which are trying to capture it.

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: The Storm Hawks are sharing the spotlight on glitzy Terra Neon - but not by choice. Two evil producers have taken the Terra captive and are forcing everyone to audition for their next big show. And unless they can find a star, it’s curtains!

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: It’s Master Mayhem’s birthday! But what do you get for the galactic overlord who has everything? TOTAL CONTROL OVER THE EARTH! Now Dome Diddy Dome is coming to crush us all, and make Master Mayhem’s birthday wish come true.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Soldier
programme.desc: The next minicon alert is from Antarctica. Starscream retrieves the minicon, but has it stolen from him. Hot Shot learns a lesson in cooperation.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: When a wanna-be rock star is accidentally transformed into a Shark -- he also becomes the overnight musical success, now known as "Rox"!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: When the Mayor of Megalopolis takes ill, the Vice-Mayor takes over for him. Unfortunately, the Vice Mayor turns out to be none other than SKOR. As soon as Skor assumes his mayoral duties, he immediately starts taking advantage of his power.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Our heroes have only the Paradwyn stone left to acquire. The Book of Elders informs the kids that the only being who can help them find the jungle realm’s Dreamstone is "The Ultimate Dream Creature" – a majestic beast called a Manticore.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: We open on yet another instance of the Posse Combosizing and defeating Skor. Desperate to win next time, Skor creates his own gadget that will allow him to Combosize himself and Skrap. However, the device barely works.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Edgar excitedly shows the posse his new telescope. He plans to use it to watch “Bailey’s Comet. ” It’s a brilliant comet streaking through the sky – making its closest pass to earth in nearly 60 million years.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Down for the Count
programme.desc: Gore’s looking to suplex Playmaker into submission, but Playmaker’s not about to tap out!

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: From Don Cheadle to Lou Diamond Phillips to Playmate of the year Brande Roderick to Meatloaf to Mickey Rooney -- the stars all showed up for the opportunity to play with the greatest poker players of all time at the WPT invitational.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: Players are on board a luxury cruise in the Caribbean and playing for hundreds of thousands of dollars.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: The biggest little city in the world plays host to the World Poker Challenge at the Reno Hilton and the last chance to win a guaranteed seat in the WPT Championship.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: The World Poker Tour travels across the globe to crown new champions.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: On this special edition episode Lou Diamond Phillips joins the WPT for a poker crash course.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: In a special, invitation-only event at the Bicycle Casino in Los Angeles, six of the hottest women in poker battle it out in the World Poker Tour arena.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: It's a legendary final in Hollywood as six of poker's best line up in Los Angeles for a shot at the 1,500,000-prize pool and one of poker's most prestigious titles.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: The stars are out in Hollywood for the WPT Hollywood Home Game.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: WPT Commentator Mike Sexton is joined by world famous poker pros Daniel Negreanu, Annie Duke and Phil Hellmuth.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: Two of the Discovery Networks most popular shows face off for a night of exciting poker action on a special episode of the World Poker Tour.
programme.sub-title: WPT American Chopper vs. Trading Spaces 2005

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: Luck is a lady once again as the finest female players in poker are back to battle it out in Ladies Night II.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 15
programme.desc: Love is in the air as Glen rids Riley of her Valentine's issues, Jay fixes Leon's bedroom problems, Alice works on Suzanne's cocky Cupid, and Sketch divorces Jordan from his tattoo.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 1
programme.desc: The team tackle Sam's embarrassing tattoo, which has earned him a serious ultimatum, deal with holiday ink that Ryan doesn't remember getting, and rid Adam of a lasting reminder of his ex.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 3
programme.desc: Glamour model Bex wants to put her Jedward tattoo behind her, and Andy has an inspirational tattoo story. Annie hopes to fix a tattoo that, after a mid-inking disagreement, didn't turn out as planned.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: Crash the Bachelorette
programme.desc: Jenni and Angelina clear the air, and Operation Crash the Bachelorette is in full effect as Mike, Vinny, and Pauly hatch a plan to crash the girls' weekend in NOLA.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: Beignets, Blow Up Dolls and Bridesmaids
programme.desc: Jenni is coming for Vinny's head, and is that a bird, a plane or The Designation?
programme.sub-title: Beignets, Blow Up Dolls and Bridesmaids

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: P-WOWW
programme.desc: The guys' takeover of Angelina's bachelorette party starts with the strip club and ends with Pauly and Jenni in the same bed.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: So That Happened!
programme.desc: As the countdown to Angelina's wedding begins, Mike is moving on up and Pauly D and Jenni's wild night has the roomies asking a lot of questions.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Liam's Final Stir of the Pot
programme.desc: With Ricci causing shockwaves, a divide forms in the group. Ash loses it with Liam and Dan threatens to lose it with Ricci. Ricci and Vicky attempt some form of closure.
programme.sub-title: Liam's Final Stir of the Pot

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Kayleigh's Crisis
programme.desc: Get ready for more sun-soaked drama, as another set of sexy singles head to paradise for a summer of love... or so they think. This time, the drama is set to be more explosive than ever!

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Love Triangles And Squares
programme.desc: The arrival of two exes is causing chaos in the Villa, and it's only going to get worse. Rogan's romance with Anita is about to be ruined when ex Jess arrives on the beach of doom.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: I'm Sorry, I'm A Prick
programme.desc: Five exes are in the villa and everyone is on edge with the arrival of Gaz from Geordie Shore. Gary makes his move on Anita, they fall for each other and her Ex emerges from the sea.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Star Gazing
programme.desc: Joe's arrival stuns the villa and Anita is in pieces. Kayleigh tells Joe that Anita has been getting it on with two boys in the house. The next Ex is a familiar face. Welcome Charlotte!

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: The Gloves Are Off
programme.desc: Jess finds out about Rogan's kiss with Megan and takes revenge by kissing Morgan. Meanwhile Adam has now moved on to Charlotte, and her and Kayleigh fall out, big time.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 4
programme.desc: Michael needs help with a holiday tattoo that's writing cheques his anatomy can't cash. Superfan Emma wants to commit to ink her love for Cheryl Fernandez-Versini. A fire-eater lights up Jay's eyes.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 5
programme.desc: Lou helps tomboy Lucy, who has a vulgar phrase tattooed on her leg. Skater boy Matt shows Sketch a tattoo in an unsightly place. Jay assists Amina, who thinks getting inked is worse than giving birth.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 6
programme.desc: Sketch meets Gaz, who has his best friend's initials in an intimate place, and the whole team are shocked by the cover-up he wants. Jay gives Lady Gaga superfan Harry the portrait of his dreams.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 7
programme.desc: Jay deals with a couple who are obsessed with going on holiday and getting cheap tattoos. Lou is busy sorting out Thom, who has arrived with a rude tribute to one of his friends on his rear end.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 8
programme.desc: Victoria is in desperate need of a tattoo cover-up so she can train as a teacher. Abigail gets inked at the age of 75, Hollie loses her cheeky chicken ink, and Lisa divorces a big tattoo mistake.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 1
programme.desc: Vicky celebrates her family with an animal tribute tattoo, Abby's cringe-worthy celebrity crush is covered, Jordan needs to lose the ink he got to win an argument, and Adam has a rather personal tatt.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 2
programme.desc: Respectable dad Stephen aims to see the back of a crude tattoo he had 20 years ago. Tattoo-mad ex-solider Rob's phallic self-portrait is a step too far, and Leeanneah has lyrics tattooed on her chest.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 194
programme.desc: Can a bullet ricochet off pavement and up through a car's floorboard, as seen in "Burn Notice"? Can you ride an explosion's shock wave to jump farther than you can under your own power?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 131
programme.desc: Can a sonic shock wave shatter glass? Is it possible to bend bullets around obstacles, like in the movie "Wanted?"

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 12
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a bullet cause explosive decompression and tear a plane apart? Will a bullet used as a fuse in a vehicle work, and then will it fire?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 31
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a toy car beat a Dodge Viper in a quarter-mile downhill race? Is yawning contagious? Does buttered toast always land buttered-side down?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 84
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to beat a speed-tracking camera? Can a defibrillator cause nitroglycerin patches to explode?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 155
programme.desc: Can a sneeze leave a person's nose at around 100mph? Can sneeze droplets travel up to 30 feet? Can nasal secretions contaminate a room quickly? Can you be decapitated by a tornado?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 67
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a hybrid rocket be propelled with salami? Is it more efficient to drive a truck with its tailgate up than down? Can a sword slice a machine gun barrel?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 199
programme.desc: Is the method used for boarding planes by most airlines inefficient? Can you make an untraceable bullet out of teeth?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 191
programme.desc: Can a fire extinguisher hold off a flamethrower? Can a fleet of cars kick up enough dust to blind a surveillance drone?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 85
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is there any way to prevent a bloodhound from tracking one's scent? Is it possible to get past a guard dog? Can an old dog be taught new tricks?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 157
programme.desc: Is there any way to prevent a bloodhound from tracking one's scent? Is it possible to fool a dog trained to sniff out contraband?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 124
programme.desc: Can a banana peel really cause one to slip? Does double-dipping cause germ warfare? Can you really make a homemade diamond?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 184
programme.desc: Will an explosion in a sewer launch a manhole cover as seen in many Hollywood films? Can truck bedliner withstand a car crash, a dog bite and an explosion?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 97
programme.desc: Myths tested: Are bulls really aggravated by the color red? Can ammunition stored in the oven be lethal? Do bulls in a china shop really cause complete destruction?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 102
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to use a rowboat as a submarine? Can one escape if buried in the sand? What kind of projectiles would pirates use in a cannon if they run out of cannonballs?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 110
programme.desc: Does cabin fever exist? Does accelerating into a moose cause less damage than braking? Can a single stick of dynamite with a 20-second fuse be thrown, retrieved by a dog and detonate under an SUV?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 94
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a baseball be hit hard enough to remove its covering? Does sliding into a base get a person there faster? Can balls be hit further with a corked bat?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 86
programme.desc: Every so often, the MythBusters draw conclusions that leave the fans seething. It’s time for the MythBusters to put more of their most contentious outcomes under the microscope.

programme.title: Superhero Movie
programme.desc: An orphaned teenager is bitten by a radioactive dragonfly, granting him superhuman powers in this parody of films like Spider-Man, X-Men, and Fantastic Four.

programme.title: Simply Irresistible
programme.desc: Magic and laughter are the main ingredients in this SIMPLY IRRESISTIBLE love story of a restaurant owner/chef and a department store executive who seem to have nothing in common.

programme.title: Ghost World
programme.desc: The story of neo-cool Enid and Rebecca who, faced with graduation from high school take a hard look at the world they wryly observe. When Rebecca focuses her attention on mutual romantic fixation Josh, the girls friendship is forever changed.

programme.title: Adult World
programme.desc: From the producer of Assassination Nation, this dark and racy satirical drama sees eccentric college graduate Amy (Emma Roberts: We’re the Millers) begrudgingly accept a job at a sex shop, all while she pursues a relationship with her teacher.

programme.title: Fast Food Nation
programme.desc: The hit satirical mockumentary taking a deep dive into the health issues and social consequences of America's love affair with fast food.

programme.title: What's the Worst That Could Happen?
programme.desc: Kevin is a thief who breaks into Max Fairbanks' unoccupied mansion. Kevin finds himself face to face with Max. And just as the cops arrest Kevin, Max claims Kevin's ring as his own.

programme.title: Aloha
programme.desc: A celebrated military contractor returns to the site of his greatest career triumphs and re-connects with a long-ago love while unexpectedly falling for the hard-charging Air Force watchdog assigned to him.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: Space hero Lone Starr (Bill Pullman) and his half-canine buddy Barf (John Candy) are on a mission in their flying Winnebago to save Princess Vespa (Daphne Zuniga) from the clutches of Dark Helmet (Rick Moranis) in Mel Brooks' inspired sci-fi spoof!

programme.title: Big Momma's House
programme.desc: In order to protect a woman from a robber, a male FBI agent disguises himself as an elderly woman.

programme.title: 10 Years
programme.desc: 10 Years follows a group of friends on their high school reunion. Among them is Jake (Channing Tatum) who is deeply in love with his girlfriend and ready to propose until he runs into his high school flame (Rosario Dawson).

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mutinous Penguins
programme.desc: Lost in the Arctic seas, Duckula, Igor and Nanny must survive in order to find their home.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Remotely Pink
programme.desc: When Pink orders a new remote control, he doesn't expect it to manipulate everything except the television.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Mayhem steals an electrical power absorbing machine and proceeds to rob Japan of its power.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Astro Duck
programme.desc: Duckula's new computer causes problems for TRASH and ends up landing the castle on the moon.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Arctic Circles
programme.desc: Duckula travels to the North Pole.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Mask enters the Baja race. But when Miles Mayem shows up for the race in Buzzard, it's apparent this is more than just a mere race.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pink Magic
programme.desc: When Pink Panther brings a new magic act into town, the tricks are on his magician competition, Big Nose.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Lifestyles of the Pink and Famous': When valet Pinky saves the life of a zillionaire, he's given a million dollar reward. 'Happy Trails Pinky': Pinky's stage coach company is threatened by the transcontinental railroad and a not too bright robber.
programme.sub-title: Lifestyles Of The Pink And Famous

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: This is the original story that explains how racing came to be an integral part of the Mask-Venom relationship: Miles Mayem decided to allow his brother Max to join Venom.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Because of a Latin class assignment, Bill and Ted decide to travel to ancient Rome to acquire a rare coin that was minted during the reign of Julius Caesar. While there, they end up saving Caesar's life.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Panthergeist': The Panther is assigned to remove a fastidious ghost from a castle. 'Pinky's Pending Pink Slip': The Panther has to think up new ways to teach biology to a problem student without the Principal catching him breaking the rules.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Unpacking from a holiday in Mexico, Penfold discovers El Loco hiding in his suitcase. El Loco gets loose and goes on a crime spree in London.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Vampire Strikes Back
programme.desc: Duckula collects enough Crunchie Nut tokens to get a Space explorers helmet.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Built For Speed': When Pinky and Rex Barklay vie for car salesmen of the month, Pinky discovers his rival is stealing new cars. 'The Pooch and the Panther': Dog groomer Pinky tries to resuscitate a canine who appears to have bought the big burrito.
programme.sub-title: Built For Speed/The Pooch And The Panther

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Hook, Line and Pinker': Pink's restful fishing vacation doesn't last long when the lake's big fish turns out to be the Loch Ness Monster. 'Valentine Pink': When Pinky shoots off three of Cupid's arrows, all heck breaks loose in Fairy Tale Land.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'A Hard Days Pink': When Pinky's fairy god manager transforms five animals into a piano, Pinky tries to win the big battle of the bands contest. 'You Only Pink Twice': Secret agent Panther must deliver a top-secret valise to the president.
programme.sub-title: A Hard Days Pink/You Only Pink Twice

programme.title: Count Duckula: Jungle Duck
programme.desc: Duckula wins a set of encyclopedias and he, Igor and Nanny travel to the jungles of darkest Africa to find the Lost Temple.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold head to the Indian Ocean to investigate several ship sinkings caused by "ghost buses". The buses turn out to be ice blocks shaped like a London bus created by Baron Greenback.
programme.sub-title: The Strange Case of the Ghost Bus

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold and Danger Mouse are shocked to find a giant spider in their bathroom.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted take a break from their chores by going on a cruise and they wind up on the Santa Maria, Christopher Columbus's ship. Columbus is so offended by these future Americans that he decides not to discover the New World and heads back to Spain.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback puts Danger Mouse and Penfold in his dream machine, where Penfold's random thoughts become reality, usually to the detriment of Danger Mouse.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When Baron Greenback threatens to take down a tracking station, Danger Mouse and Penfold head to the Bermuda Triangle to stop him. But space aliens abduct them, hoping to probe them for further research.
programme.sub-title: Close Encounters of the Absurd Kind

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Monks have the golden antlers which belonged to the stag that the goddess Artemis rode.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM steals a patchwork quilt and fireplace screen from the Smithsonian.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'From Hair to Eternity': It takes a hair styling Pink to save the day when a giant hairball wreaks havoc on the city. 'Strike Flea, You're Out,' the Panther becomes a baseball superstar through the inadvertent help of a little, itch-inducing flea.
programme.sub-title: From Hair To Eternity/Strike Flea, You're Out

programme.title: Count Duckula: Autoduck
programme.desc: Duckula tries to create a world record for the world's fastest car.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are chosen as ambassadors to meet space aliens.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM holds Tokyo for ransom, threatening to cause Mt. Fuji to erupt with its magma mole – a machine that digs huge tunnels and is capable of setting off earthquakes.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold go to the Amazon jungle where a tribe have been sacrificing people to The Great Ant God from Ataxia. Penfold is captured by the tribe and his "magic caterpillars" (eyebrows) are to be sacrificed.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: On an archeological dig, Matt and scientists come upon a mysterious gorge where their cars have been crushed.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Ghost and Mr. Panther': Pinky unknowingly takes a job as a house sitter…at a haunted house! 'Cleopanthra': Pinky wants to make it as an architect in ancient Egypt but must contend with the Royal Architect who is out to destroy his work.

programme.title: Count Duckula
programme.desc: Duckula tries to impress the nanny fan club by winning all the winter sports.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold types up his memoirs. Colonel K tells them that things around the city are suddenly vanishing with a loud "kazonk". Then Danger Mouse and Penfold are kazonked into a void-like dimension by Dr. Crumhorn.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: '14 Karat Pink': The Panther tries to claim the Lollapalooza Gold Vein but Manly Man has other ideas. 'Robo-Pink': While working as a Security Guard at a super secret factory, the Panther thwarts the Dogfather's plans to steal a secret 'doodad.'

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Matt discovers an empty submarine controlled by Brian the Brain, a computer he had given grant money for, and now in the clutches of Mayhem and the VENOM gang.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted find themselves with a problem: how to get part-time jobs when they have no experience. They manage to get references from Marie Antoinette and Lucretia Borgia but decide that working on music is preferable to working at a real job.
programme.sub-title: A Job, A Job, My Kingdom For A Job

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Cowboy Pink': Panther helps Heidi Von Schmarty get her herd of cows to San Francisco, despite cattle rustling attempts by Dogfather. 'Stealth Panther': Panther goes on a secret mission to recover a stolen Stealth aircraft from a group of bad guys.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has a device that transmits waves to brainwash people. He brainwashes Colonel K to send Danger Mouse and Penfold on a mission to the South Pole, where they have trouble with a mechanical wild goose that lays explosive eggs.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: During an investigation where Danger Mouse and Penfold try to find out why elephants are being turned into sugar cubes, a fall causes Penfold to act weird and do a Tarzan yell that summons elephants to stampede the area.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: The Australian Outback is the setting for a true Battle of the Giants. The Mask Team is pitted against a Venom Team.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Voodoo Man': When Voodoo Man's island is wiped out by a storm, he goes in search of the weatherman he believes is responsible. '7 Manly Men': When the toy in a box of cereal doesn't live up to its promise, the Manly Men confront the company owner.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Matt, Scott and T-Bob's trip on the Orient Express is interrupted when three doors are stolen from the compartments that used to belong to King Ludwig.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 21
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a terraced house in Derby, a property in Kent and a cottage in rural Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 14
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a house in Devon with some interesting wallpaper, a marvellous flat in Kensal Green and a valuable bit of land in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander view properties in Plymouth, Buckinghamshire and London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 16
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a cottage in Cornwall, a two bedroomed flat in Hampshire and property in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 15
programme.desc: Martin has found a property in Plymouth that's money for jam...in Kent - swirling patterns are on the walls and ceilings - is it art or artex! Finally, they won't need a bow and arrow to target the problem with a cottage in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Kent, Essex and Markham.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view properties in North London, Folkestone and Plymouth.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 10
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin see a two bedroom, mid terrace in Fenton, Stoke on Trent; in Earlsfield, South London there's a ground floor, garden flat in need of some serious redecoration work; and a mid terrace, two bedroom house in Folkestone on the Kent coast.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 3
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit an old mill in Derby, a terraced house in Middlesex and a two story maisonette in Clydbank.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 30
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a bungalow in Cheshire, a semi detached house in London and a Victorian mid-terrace in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Wales, South Hampton and East London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 18
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts travel to Wiltshire to see a four bedroom box house with lots of space to play with; Devon offers a grey 2 listed house and its pink! In Stoke-on-Trent your getting two for the price of one!

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 6
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a plot of land in Ripon, find a problem behind a wardrobe in Kent and an old Ambulance hall in Mid Glamorgan.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 26
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts go to Helston in Cornwall in search of the perfect rural retreat, then to Crystal Palace to view a two bedroom basement flat and finally they go to Bury to look at a house which may be the worst on a very nice street.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit a two bedroom house in North London.

programme.title: Cyrano De Bergerac
programme.desc: The charismatic swordsman-poet helps another woo the woman he loves in this straightforward version of the play.

programme.title: Over the Top
programme.desc: A Championship title, a huge cash award, and his son's love -- the greatest prize of all -- are at stake when Hawk battles his strongest enemy for everything he has ever wanted.

programme.title: Hackers
programme.desc: A neophyte hacker becomes the target of the FBI after he unknowingly taps into a high-tech embezzling scheme which could cause a horrific environmental disaster in this suspenseful drama.

programme.title: Vampire Hookers
programme.desc: Carradine is an aged vampire who has a bevy of vampiric beauties who lure many of their customers back to his lair. A pair of virile young Navy sailors get mixed up in their shenanigans.

programme.title: Poltergeist II: The Other Side
programme.desc: Those ghastly ghosts return to battle once more with JoBeth Williams and Craig T. Nelson for the soul of a young clairvoyant.

programme.title: Poltergeist III
programme.desc: In the climactic finale to the Poltergeist trilogy, those elusive demons are more frightening than ever as they follow Carol Anne to Chicago and inhabit an entire skyscraper.

programme.title: The Ape
programme.desc: Dr. Bernard Adrian is a kindly mad scientist who seeks to cure a young woman's polio. He needs spinal fluid from a human to complete the formula for his experimental serum.

programme.title: Masters Of The Universe
programme.desc: The fantastic exploits of He-Man and his arch rival, the evil Skeletor, are brought to the big screen for the first time in this live-action adventure.

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: A man in London tries to help a counter-espionage Agent. But when the Agent is killed, and the man stands accused, he must go on the run to save himself and stop a spy ring that is trying to steal top-secret information.

programme.title: Killer Klowns From Outer Space
programme.desc: One-of-a-kind alien invasion sci-fi featuring blood drinking alien clowns harvesting a small city.

programme.title: A Chorus Line
programme.desc: After narrowing down hundreds of Broadway hopefuls, choreographer Zach (Michael Douglas) leads a select group of dancers on an audition that could change their lives forever in this stunning adaptation of the hit Broadway musical.

programme.desc: Euronews offers a unique perspective on world events. It empowers people to make up their own mind by delivering factual analysis and a diversity of viewpoints. Because all views matter, Euronews is "All Views".

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Women who kill husbands, lovers, rivals and parents will shock society. But a very special horror is reserved for women who will kill their own.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women never get their hands dirty: no blood touched them because they manipulate others to kill for them...but they didn't get away.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: In some cases, the transformation from everyday woman to brutal killer is activated by a man. A boyfriend or husband can become the focus of a deadly obsession.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women burn madly with jealousy, an anger so deep they’ll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn from “green with envy” to “lethal with jealousy?" Find out as we explore their hearts of darkness.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Under His Control
programme.desc: These are love stories with bleak, bloody endings. Blinded by desire, obsessed and malleable, some women fall into lethal relationships with dangerous, controlling men.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: No one doubts a woman’s capacity to love, but sometimes lust can turn lethal. In the ultimate fatal attraction, "Deadly Women" looks at cases of murder driven by obsessive love.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Women are more often prey than predators, but the exceptions are deadly women indeed. In this episode, we’ll discover what drove these deadly women to prey on the unsuspecting.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Greed is one of the oldest motives for murder, and these deadly women are no exception: they’ll do anything to satisfy their need for cash. This is the dangerous world of the fortune hunters.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Greed
programme.desc: Known as “black widows,” these women kill their spouses, lovers and children for profit. Murder for them becomes a business. Their trade is murder. Their profit? Blood money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Crimes of revenge are usually single acts of passion. A brew of hatred and restitution boils over in a manic moment of fury. Once caught, revenge killers usually display remorse and atonement.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Blood ties are strong ties, sometimes strong enough to pull a person into a murderous conspiracy. When a family member wields his or her power, the result can be death.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While their sisters chose a life of sensible, suburban motherhood, these deadly women chose a life of crime. They lived with the thrills, the danger and the deadly consequences of being outlaws.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Married to Murder
programme.desc: To the outside world, their marriages seemed happy. But behind closed doors, there was no domestic bliss. These women wanted out, but getting a divorce wasn’t their preferred option.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: We want to believe that all women would give their lives to protect their children. But there are horrifying cases when a mother will turn against her natural instinct and kill her own flesh and blood.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women would stop at nothing to get their hands on the cash they needed. From husbands to strangers to other people’s babies, they spilt blood for money.

programme.title: The FBI Files: .22 Caliber Killer
programme.desc: In Buffalo, New York, a sniper targeted African-American men. He held a city hostage, and threatened to ignite a racial powder keg. And it looked like others might be following his bloody example.

programme.title: The FBI Files: A Model Killer
programme.desc: It's a model's job to rivet the public's attention. But in 1984, some aspiring models began attracting publicity in a terrible new way.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Hidden Agenda
programme.desc: Across the Midwest, a band of armed robbers struck banks at the rate of nearly one per month. They attacked with lightning precision and lethal force. They were in and out in five minutes.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Unabomber
programme.desc: For nearly two decades a mathematical genius with delusions of single-handedly destroying industrial society, planted or mailed powerful bombs to unsuspecting innocent victims.

programme.title: The FBI Files: When Seconds Count
programme.desc: On July 2nd 1994, a 12-year-old girl was abducted from her home in northern California. The FBI knows to act fast, search wide, enlist volunteers. But as the hours slipped by, agents also realized their chances for recovering her alive grew slim.

programme.title: The FBI Files: A Stranger in Town
programme.desc: She appeared on the highway in the spring, a young woman, her life cut short, her past and her identity a mystery. Only by finding out who she was could we ever hope to find her killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Despite what you see in the movies, most bank robbers come in quietly, pass a note, and slip away. They want cash, not bloodshed. But in Richmond, Virginia, a pair of gunmen entered a bank with their guns blazing.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Polly Klaas: Kidnapped
programme.desc: On October 1, 1993, a stranger abducted 12-year-old Polly Klaas during a slumber party in her home.

programme.title: The FBI Files: John Gotti: Convicted
programme.desc: John Gotti was ambitious, powerful and ruthless. He ruled with flash and conspicuous style, playing celebrity with the media and cat and mouse with the FBI. They called John Gotti the Teflon Don. Serious charges just wouldn't stick.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Killer Instinct
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, a serial killer stalked lone victims in the dead of night, his hunting ground the suburbs of Washington, D.C., his target, anyone who owned a car.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Killing Spree
programme.desc: In 1984, in South Florida, a rapist and murderer was on the loose. The killer was a ruthless and terrifying predator, the newest member of an infamous group known as serial killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a tragic murder led agents into the frightening world of domestic terrorism. Law enforcement vowed to dismantle the violent faction that claimed responsibility.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Crazy Don
programme.desc: To the FBI, he was believed to be the boss of New York's most powerful mafia organization: the Genovese crime family. Was Gigante crazy or was his behavior a shrewd attempt to disguise his position as a ruthless mafia boss?

programme.title: The FBI Files: A Family Torn
programme.desc: The parents of 11 yr. old Brittany Martinez knew their daughter was no runaway. Something had happened to her, something terrible. When they failed to find Brittany in their quiet Illinois neighborhood, they called the police.

programme.title: The FBI Files: First and Kennedy Street Crew
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, Washington, D.C., became the murder capital of the nation. Few were left untouched by gang violence, which was ignited by the drug trade. By 1994, that violence would strike at the very heart of law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Silent Strike
programme.desc: Money may not be the root of all evil, but for some men, a million dollars is enough to justify murder. Even men sworn to uphold the law can sometimes be tempted.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Under Fire
programme.desc: A series of armored car heists and bloody shoot-outs shocked law enforcement. The brutal violence of the crimes suggested a motive more complex than greed. When the robbers started targeting police, the FBI took charge.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Carter receives a Dear John letter and wants to join the rest of the prisoners that escape and go home.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan tricks Klink into calling forth a bomber for study, but it arrives with a complication - a German general familiar with Hogan's style.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Klink reluctantly falls in with two other Kommandants plotting against Burkhalter. Burkhalter finds out. Now Hogan must step in before Stalag 13 loses Klink the military way - by firing squad.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan's operation is known to a Gestapo man who wants to trade his knowledge for $1,000,000 in diamonds, but can he be trusted?

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Efforts to aid a Nazi general's assassination plan hit a snag when Schultz unknowingly activates a time bomb meant for the Führer.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: As the Hammelburg Underground lodges a complaint against Hogan's success, Klink finds himself caught between the Gestapo and the Wehrmacht, each trying to take over Stalag 13.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan is stymied over how to rescue a captured member of the underground until a potential diversion appears in the form of an old WWI buddy of Sgt. Schultz.
programme.sub-title: The Rise And Fall Of Sergeant Schultz

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Rusted-out plumbing collapses the emergency tunnel just as Hogan needs to get four Underground leaders out of Germany, so he creates a health spa.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: All goes well when Hogan's gang steals Klink's code book from his safe, photographs the contents, and returns it to its proper place. But there's a small hitch - Carter forgets to load the camera with film.
programme.sub-title: A Klink, A Bomb And A Short Fuse

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: When Newkirk discovers a new radio-controlled tank that could win the war for Germany, the allies want Hogan and the men to photograph the tank and then destroy it - but complications ensue during their mission.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Ate the Evidence

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Lady Killer
programme.desc: Investigators find the parallels between the two cases troubling: two young, isolated and vulnerable Guyanese women, two deaths in strange circumstances, two life insurance policies. Will Mohammed Khan escape justice again?

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Bathtub Sisters
programme.desc: The mother of two teenaged girls is found dead in her bathtub. The coroner declares it an accidental death, but a year later, new information prompt the police to reopen the case.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Mother's Intuition
programme.desc: 14-year-old Pamela Bischoff misses her curfew on April 12, 1991. Though she has only been missing a few hours, her mother, Gail, tells the Oromocto RCMP she is certain Pamela has been raped and murdered by an older boy named Billy Stillman.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Winston's Secret
programme.desc: In April 1996, Winston Malcolm is rescued from icy waters of a lake after a canoe accident. He announced to hospital staff that his wife was with him. A dive team is called to the rescue, and the body of Jan Marshall is finally found a few days later.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Friend or Foe
programme.desc: Jutta Weber is last seen on August 6, 1997. In August 1999, Don Gazley is arrested and wants to cut a deal: if the police make his charges go away, he’ll tell them where Jutta Weber’s body is. Is Gazley telling the truth?

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Bad Trip
programme.desc: On August 20, 2001, Charles Tremblay, 15, was beaten, then thrown into the St-Charles River where he drowned. Tremblay's body was discovered in the mud less than 24 hours after his death.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: House of Horrors
programme.desc: The body of a woman is found floating next to a dock. The case takes the detectives into a sinister world from which few come out unharmed.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Great Flood
programme.desc: Almost every culture on Earth tells the story of an ancient flood – details vary but the basic plot is the same - An angry God wipes out an unrepentant population.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact
programme.desc: Throughout history, the earth has been bombarded from space. Asteroid impacts have caused mass extinctions – including the dinosaur wipe out.
programme.sub-title: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact

programme.title: Naked Science: Great Lakes
programme.desc: The Great Lakes are sometimes referred to as inland seas; they are the largest freshwater system in the world. Naked Science examines the early history of these amazing bodies

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Terry's Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Terry and Larry tackle the epic Lethal Weapon series testing 4 different scenes.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Bond-ing with Larry
programme.desc: Who is the greatest international action hero of all time, Terry Schappert?

programme.desc: Terry and Larry test a famous scene from X-men. They are attempting to see if you can split a bullet with a Katana sword.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Getting to Know Rick O'Shea
programme.desc: In this episode we get know Larry’s friend Rick O’Shea (Ricochet).

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Terry Alone at Home
programme.desc: Who left Terry Home Alone! Larry did. Terry and Larry test several scenes from the classic film series Home Alone.

programme.desc: Terry and Larry will test different famous shots from westerns. Can you shoot a cowboy hat off the bad guy?

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Schapbo

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Shark Go Boom
programme.desc: In this iconic scene, Chief Brody hangs on to his sinking boat as a giant shark approaches him with a scuba tank lodged in its mouth.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Terry's Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A murderer tries to kill his final victim as the car they are in slowly sinks to the bottom of a lake.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Boat made out of ice; comets; science behind hypothermia.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Avoiding jetlag; a demonstration of the size of the solar system; square-wheeled motorbike.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Understanding the devastating Japanese earthquake.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Jem builds a laser; Yan recreates a demonstration to calculate the speed of light.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The future of recycling; how GPS works and how it is changing the world; using science to crack a magic trick.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The origins of weather; seasons allow life to form; Jem checks out wave power in Scotland.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Investigating the 2010 Gulf of Mexico oil disaster; Liz takes part in wildlife rescue efforts.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Homemade gules to test stickiness; puzzle with a scientific optical illusion; supersonic car.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: EARTHQUAKES
programme.desc: Earthquakes happen when tectonic plates suddenly slip causing shock waves to shake the surface of the Earth. They occur on fault lines - the most active are the Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean and the San Andreas fault on America’s west coast.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MEGAFLOODS
programme.desc: Water covers two thirds of the earth’s surface, and we are utterly dependent on it, but when this vital ingredient of life unleashes its fury on the planet it becomes the ultimate bringer of death.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: CONSTRUCTION
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels have transformed the way we live, making the inaccessible easy to reach, and turning time consuming journeys into convenient commutes, but when things go wrong they can go very wrong indeed.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: AIR, LAND AND SEA
programme.desc: Planes, trains and ships, our mass transit systems, have had a profound effect on mankind, making the world smaller and more connected.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: HURRICANES
programme.desc: Hurricanes, Typhoons and Cyclones all the same thing with different names – what they also have in common is their destructive power! Hurricanes are large, swirling storms and winds from a hurricane can damage buildings or trees.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Planet X
programme.desc: The New Horizons mission continues deeper into space, it’s next target looming close.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Hyabusa
programme.desc: The Japanese Asteroid sample return mission has reached its target and is surveying the space rock ready to dip down and take a sample.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Fresh Eyes
programme.desc: Two new space telescopes are in orbit and completing their shakedown; Gaia and TESS they too will be joined by the James Webb Telescope.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Martian Attractions
programme.desc: The Latest in Martian exploration from orbit and on the ground, new sources of water and planetary dust storms are a few of the stories unfolding on to the Red Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Heavy Lift
programme.desc: New heavy lift rockets are taking to the sky. Falcon Heavy, SLS, Arianne 6 and Vega C rockets are primed to begin the next phase of space expansion.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Proving Einstein
programme.desc: Some of the proof of Einstein’s theories had long been elusive, beyond the capabilities of technology, until now.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Where Are All the Aliens?
programme.desc: We’re looking hard, but the numbers aren’t stacking up, is life on other planets that hard to find? Even signals from an advanced race elude us.

programme.title: STAR LIGHT, STAR BRIGHT
programme.desc: The two current science missions to our star; the sun have made great leaps forward in the science of how stars work, how they generate solar winds and how they affect their orbiting planets

programme.title: MOON SHINE
programme.desc: The International drive to return to the Moon is gathering pace, this multi- nation effort is readying to return, stay and utilise the Lunar assets.

programme.title: RISING TIDES
programme.desc: The next generation of Earth observation satellites are now in place maintaining the continuity of long-term climate data, in particular the planets rising temperatures and related ocean levels.

programme.title: EARTH´S EVIL TWIN
programme.desc: Venus is a planet closest to Earth in distance and size, it once may have been as hospitable to life as Earth is now. Then something changed and Venus became shrouded in hot dense toxic clouds.

programme.title: The New Frontier: INCREASING ORBIT
programme.desc: The ISS is the workhorse of the international spacefaring community, but its days are numbered. R

programme.title: The New Frontier: GIANTS IN THE DARK
programme.desc: Asteroids, the debris from the early solar system hold tantalizing clues to planetary formation. The spacecraft Psyche readies to study a unique metallic asteroid.

programme.title: The New Frontier: OCEANS IN THE SKY
programme.desc: Juice and the Europa Clipper are two missions to our Solar System’s largest planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: WEBB OF LIFE
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope is now operational. Launched aboard an Arianne 5 the 10-billion-dollar spacecraft has deployed successfully and reached its orbit around the Sun.

programme.title: The New Frontier: PEERING INTO THE ABYSS
programme.desc: Imaging a black hole was a breakthrough in astronomy, now with the new satellite IXPE high energy telescope we will be looking at some of the most dramatic, and most violent objects in the universe: Colliding Black holes and Neutron Stars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: THE NEXT BIG LEAPS
programme.desc: Projects in the works. SunRISE, SPHEREx, NEOS and others, their mission to expand our knowledge of Earth, the surrounding space, Dark Matter, and the earliest galaxies. N

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Cold War & The Start of the Space Race - Cold War rivalry and the acceleration of space exploration. Servicing Hubble - Preparations for Hubble's final servicing mission. Chasing a Comet - Launch and the difficult path to rendezvous.
programme.sub-title: The Space Race, The Hubble, Chasing Comets & Space Debris

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Mercury Begins - The start of NASA & America's first manned space program. Mars Express - The European Space Agency's Mars Satellite. The Soyuz Launch System - The gradual evolution of Russia's Soyuz launcher.
programme.sub-title: Project Mercury, The Mars Express & The Soyuz Launch System

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Mercury Advances - America's first manned space program finally puts a man in orbit. Camera Tracking - The array of special cameras that follow a spacecraft at launch. The International Space Station - The building and operation.
programme.sub-title: Project Mercury Advancement, Camera Tracking & The International Space Station

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Gemini Commences - Did Gemini give the US a lead in the space race? Venus Express - The last days of ESA's Venus probe. EMU - NASA's Extravehicular Mobility Unit. Deep Space Communications - ESA's worldwide network of giant dishes.
programme.sub-title: Project Gemini, Venus Express, EMU & Deep Space Communication

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Gemini Advances - NASA refines the techniques needed for rendezvous. Cassini at Saturn - In the Cassini probe's final years of activity, its handlers are asking it to do more complex and risky assignments.
programme.sub-title: Project Gemini Advances, Cassini at Saturn & More About Mars

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Gemini: Docking and EVA - In the final phase of the Gemini program NASA struggles with docking and spacewalks. Curiosity Roving Mars - On the surface of Mars the Curiosity rover is changing the way we think about the red planet.
programme.sub-title: Project Gemini Docking, Mars Curiousity & Satellite Swarms

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Apollo Program Begins - At the very beginning of NASA's moon project things go wrong. Caves - ESA's special training program for astronauts. Planck - Mapping the cosmic microwave background radiation.
programme.sub-title: The Apollo Program Begins, Caves & Planck

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Rebuilding Project Apollo - After the Apollo 1 disaster NASA resumes manned space flight. Orbiting Carbon Observatory 2 - Monitoring the Earth's Co2 levels in unprecedented detail. Zero Gravity Training - Parabolic flights.
programme.sub-title: Rebuilding Apollo, Orbiting Carbon & Zero Gravity Training

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Beyond Earth Orbit - The Apollo program takes bold new steps. IXV Re-entry Vehicle - Europe is working to perfect a re-entry vehicle. Galileo Satellites Lost and Found - Navigation satellites go into incorrect orbit.
programme.sub-title: Beyond Earth Orbit, IXV Vehicles & Galileo Satellites Lost and Found

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: To the Moon - The Apollo program achieves its major objective. Dragon, Falcon and Spacex - New directions in re-usable spacecraft. Gaia- Mapping our galaxy in unprecedented detail.
programme.sub-title: To The Moon, SpaceX's Dragon and Falcon & GAIA

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Beginning of the End - After the first Moon landing the Apollo science program was about to start in earnest but people were losing interest. Apollo Gets Complicated - New technology is added that extends the reach of the Apollo program.
programme.sub-title: The Beginning of the End, Apollo Gets Complicated & The End of Apollo

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Shuttle Program - The first re-useable spacecraft. The Challenger Disaster - Pressing launch schedules lead to carelessness and the loss of the Challenger. Return to Space - A re-designed shuttle is back in space with a redefined role.
programme.sub-title: The Shuttle Program, The Challenger Disaster & Return to Space

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Space Shuttle Matures - The Space Transportation System changes the nature of orbital missions. Complex Construction in Space - Confidence in the shuttle begins to return and engineers for get the lessons of the past.
programme.sub-title: The Space Shuttle Matures, Complex Space Construction & The Final Years of the Space Shuttle

programme.title: World War A - When Aliens Attack

programme.desc: In this tense thriller starring John Travolta and Kate Bosworth, a team of Texas linemen races to install miles of cable before a deadly lightning storm destroys the power grid.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Scent Of The Kill
programme.desc: A dog can be a dead man's best friend. Dogs have been trained to sniff out corpses, drugs, explosives, and missing persons. They're often the first to find the essential clue that sets an investigation in motion.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Deadly Target
programme.desc: Ballistics: A corpse is found with a gunshot wound to the head; the weapon lies next to the victim. It looks like suicide, but could it be murder? It's a question best solved by ballistics experts.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Loved To Death
programme.desc: A victim usually never foresees danger when the perpetrator turns out to be a friend or a lover. Science and microscopic evidence can unmask these killers and find justice for those who are Loved To Death.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Shattered Vows
programme.desc: A woman is found dead at the bottom of the basement stairs. As detectives look into the accident, they begin to question the sequence of events.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Three hairs microscopic fibers a common trash bag ripped from a roll. Seemingly small and insignificant clues become a victim's silent witness.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Without A Trace
programme.desc: Missing Person: Approximately 1.8 million Americans are reported missing each year. Worldwide, the number of missing persons nearly triples.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Trial Of The Century
programme.desc: Forensics in the O.J. Simpson Murder Trial: Millions watched on television as the jury rendered their verdict. Orenthal James Simpson was found not guilty of murder.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Face Of Tragedy
programme.desc: Forensic Sculpting: Forensic sculptors retrieve people from oblivion. Using clay and an intricate knowledge of anatomy, forensic arts place a face on an unidentified skull, recreating the victim's likeness, which often leads to his name.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Short Fuse
programme.desc: Explosives Investigations: The crime lab is the place where science meets murder. In New York State, Eleanor Fowler opened a small package, which was mailed to her home. When she lifted the lid, the box exploded, killing her instantly.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Signed In Blood
programme.desc: Handwriting Analysis: We've all heard that our handwriting tells more about our personalities than we think. Are we risk-takers, have low-esteem, fun loving, or are we capable of murder?

programme.title: The New Detectives: Bad Medicine
programme.desc: Drug trafficking has spawned a violent and deadly criminal underground. It's providing a challenge to forensic investigators devoted to cracking drug rings.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Invisible Death
programme.desc: Poison is an almost invisible form of death, and toxicologists must look for hidden clues in blood and tissue to bring these murders to light.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Web of Clues
programme.desc: Forensic Entomology: Bugs have roamed the earth for 250 million years, but their intimate association with death is just now coming to life.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Mind Hunters

programme.title: The New Detectives: In The Line of Fire

programme.title: The New Detectives: Coroner's Casebook

programme.title: The New Detectives: Written In Bone
